title: Kettch
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Youmay|the fictional [[Ewok/Legends|Ewok]]|the [[Kettch (Endor)|real Ewok named Kettch]]}} {{Citation}} {{Character |type=GFFA |image=[[File:ToppsGS6Kettch.jpg]] |name=Kettch |homeworld=[[Endor/Legends|Endor]] |birth= |death= |species=[[Ewok/Legends|Ewok]]''[[X-Wing: Wraith Squadron]]'' |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=He/him |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=[[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|His name is Kettch, and he's an Ewok.''"
"''No.''"
"''Oh, yes. Determined to fight. You should hear him say, 'Yub, yub.' He makes it a battle cry.''"
"''Wes, assuming he could be educated up to Alliance fighter-pilot standards, an Ewok couldn't even reach an X-wing's controls.''"
"''He wears arm and leg extensions, prosthetics built for him by a sympathetic medical droid. And he's anxious to go, Commander.''"
"''Please tell me you're kidding.''"
"''Of course I'm kidding. Pilot-candidate number one is a Human female from Tatooine, Falynn Sandskimmer.|Wes Janson and Wedge Antilles|}} '''Kettch''' was a fictional [[Ewok/Legends|Ewok]] [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] serving with [[Wraith Squadron]], invented as a prank by pilot [[Wes Janson/Legends|Wes Janson]]. ==Biography== '''Kettch ''' was created by [[Wraith Squadron]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] [[Wes Janson]] during the hunt for [[Warlord/Legends|Warlord]] [[Zsinj]] as a prank, much to [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Wedge Antilles]]'s dismay. According to Janson, Kettch was an eager [[Ewok/Legends|Ewok]] fitted with prosthetics which enabled him to fly an [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing]]. After the Wraiths assumed the guise of a [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] group, the [[Hawk-bat Independent Space Force]], the prank took on life of its own. An Ewok stuffed toy began making appearances around the Hawk-bat base, in the pilots starfighter cockpits, and Antilles even found his [[TIE/IN interceptor/Legends|TIE/IN interceptor's]] comm system modified, distorting his voice to sound like an Ewok. When the Wraiths visited Zsinj's [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|''Executor''-class Star Dreadnought]] [[Iron Fist (Executor-class)|''Iron Fist'']], disguised as Hawk-bat pirates, Zsinj asked [[Garik Loran|Garik "Face" Loran]] (in the guise of General [[Garik Loran|Kargin]]) whether there was an Ewok [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] pilot among their ranks, having been informed of Antilles's analyzed transmissions. Face quickly spun a story about Kettch's origins, borrowing heavily from the real life story of [[Gamorrean/Legends|Gamorrean]] pilot [[Voort saBinring|Voort "Piggy" saBinring]], a fellow Wraith. This then led Zsinj to conclude that Kettch might be an escaped subject from Epsilon Wing of the [[Binring Biomedical Product]] facility on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Saffalore]]. Thus, during the [[Battle of Kuat (Hunt for Zsinj)|Battle of Kuat]], Antilles was forced to fly his fighter with the Kettch doll attached to his body, giving many pilots the impression that an Ewok was really flying the starfighter. At the onset of the battle, the Hawk-Bats employed the tactical maneuver known as [[Kettch's Drill]], designed by Antilles, to eliminate a number of enemy fighters before engaging in the battle's heaviest dogfighting. In the end, Antilles used the Kettch ruse to repay Janson for all his pranks. Enlisting the help of the other Wraiths, Antilles crafted an elaborate scheme which resulted in Janson appearing stark naked before the entire squadron. That was the end of Lieutenant Kettch's appearances and the Kettch-related pranks. In a way, however, the legend of Kettch lived on. His fictional story motivated Zsinj to order his scientists to create a real Ewok pilot, [[Kolot]], complete with prosthetics and the ability to speak [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]]. Kolot escaped ''Iron Fist'' with [[Gara Petothel]], and went with her to [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]]. Gara, or rather, Kirney Slane, found this ironic. "We're both lies that eventually became the truth." ==Behind the scenes== [[File:X-Ewok.png|thumb|right|250px|An Ewok warrior climbing into the cockpit of a New Republic X-wing]] {{Quote|As for Kettch, he's the focus of a sense of surrealism that is inevitable in any high-stress environment.|[[Aaron Allston]]|{{SWArchive|url=vault/books/12041998news.html|text=Rogue Leaders: An Interview with Michael Stackpole and Aaron Allston, authors of the ''Star Wars: X-Wing'' novels|author=David Pipgras|date=1998-12-04}}}} An image of an Ewok pilot from 1993's ''[[Star Wars Gamemaster Handbook]]'', predates the character of Kettch by several years. The image is in itself [[canon]] in the [[Star Wars Legends|Legends continuity]], and may have even inspired the Kettch plotline, but it is not clear whether it should be seen as a portrait of Wraith Squadron's stuffed Ewok, or whether it reflects a real Ewok pilot like [[Kolot]] or [[Tarfang]]. Author [[Chris Cassidy]] helped make a custom Kettch action figure for [[Aaron Allston]].{{WebCite|url=http://www.aaronallston.com/picturcg.html|text=Fan Art by Chris Cassidy and Patricia Gibbs|archivedate=20080110054455|nolive=1}} There was no suggestion that a [[Kettch (Endor)|real Kettch]] existed until his name was used for a [[Hasbro]] action figure in 2008, as part of an Ewok three-pack including [[Machook]] and [[Keoulkeech]]. The packaging indicates an Ewok named Kettch participated in a conflict with the [[Lahsbee/Huhk|Lahsbees]] after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]{{HasbroCite|set=Star Wars: The Legacy Collection|packtype=''Star Wars'' Comic Pack|pack=Machook, Keoulkeech & Kettch (Marvel's ''Star Wars'' #94)|altlink=https://www.rebelscum.com/TLCcomicpacksWM093.asp}}, as seen in [[Marvel Comics]]' [[Star Wars (1977) 94|''Star Wars'' 94]].[[Star Wars (1977) 94|''Star Wars'' (1977) 94]] ==Appearances== *''[[X-Wing: Wraith Squadron]]'' {{Ab|X-Wing: Wraith Squadron (unabridged audiobook)}} {{1stm|in book}} *''[[X-Wing: Iron Fist]]'' {{Ab|X-Wing: Iron Fist (unabridged audiobook)}} {{C|as a doll}} *''[[X-Wing: Solo Command]]'' {{Ab|X-Wing: Solo Command (unabridged audiobook)}} {{C|as a doll}} *''[[X-Wing: Isard's Revenge]]'' {{Imo}} *''[[The New Jedi Order: Enemy Lines I: Rebel Dream]]'' {{Imo}} ==Sources== *{{CollectorCite|4|Rogue Leaders}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Kettch }} [[Category:Aliases, nicknames, and pseudonyms]] [[Category:Ewoks]] [[Category:Males]]
